headline: Transcript: RIP QT
summary: Marc Filippino talks to Claire Jones and Kieran Smith
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘RIP QT’

Marc FilippinoGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Thursday, October 30th, and this is your FT News Briefing. Meta had tech earnings yesterday it probably wants to forget, Microsoft, not so much. And the Federal Reserve is gonna stop shrinking its balance sheet. Plus, the cable cowboy is saying howdy to retirement. I’m Marc Filippino, and here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Meta and Microsoft left investors with mixed feelings when they reported earnings yesterday. Let’s start with Meta. Revenues rose 26 per cent last quarter, but its net income fell by a whopping 83 per cent. The company said that was because of a one-off income tax charge of nearly $15bn. That charge is related to US President Donald Trump’s One Big Beautiful Bill. Meta’s share price dropped more than 7 per cent in after-hours trading. Then there was Microsoft, and this one really comes down to investor
